text: **DrJill:** Hello, I'm Dr.
**DrJill:** Jill, and {welcome|ようこそ|/ˈwelkəm/|Welcome to my home.} to Let's Learn English with Anna.
**DrJill:** A {playground|遊び場|/ˈpleɪɡraʊnd/|Kids love the playground.} is an outdoor place.
**DrJill:** We {play|遊ぶ/演じる|/pleɪ/|Let's play soccer.} and have fun at a playground.
**DrJill:** They are {great|素晴らしい/大きい|/ɡreɪt/|That's a great idea.} places for {exercise|運動|/ˈeksərsaɪz/|Exercise is good for health.}
**DrJill:** What do you do at a playground?
**DrJill:** Let's visit Anna.
**DrJill:** She {helps|助ける|/helps/|She helps her friend.} us answer this question.
**DrJill:** What do you do at a playground?
**Anna:** Hi, welcome!
**Anna:** Hi Max!
**Max:** Hello, Anna!
**Max:** I have our question for today.
**Anna:** Great!
**Anna:** What do you do at a playground?
**Anna:** I can do lots of things at a playground.
**Max:** Baana, playgrounds are for kids.
**Anna:** That is true, but everyone can have fun at a playground.
**DrJill:** There are many ways to {play|遊ぶ|/pleɪ/|Let's play together.} at a playground.
**DrJill:** You can {climb|登る|/klaɪm/|Climb the mountain.}, {swing|ブランコをこぐ|/swɪŋ/|Swing on the playground.}, {slide|すべり台を滑る|/slaɪd/|Slide down the playground.}
**DrJill:** Run or play games like soccer or basketball.
**DrJill:** Now let's hear from some friends
**DrJill:** They talk about fun things to do at a playground.
**Max:** My favorite {playground|遊び場|/ˈpleɪɡraʊnd/|Kids love the playground.} has a {skateboard|スケートボード|/ˈskeɪtbɔːrd/|Ride a skateboard.} park
**DrJill:** At the playground, I {hang|ぶら下がる|/hæŋ/|Hang the picture.} upside down from the {monkey bars|雲梯|/ˈmʌŋki bɑːrz/|Play on monkey bars.}
**DrJill:** My favorite playground has a soccer field.
**DrJill:** Thanks friends!
**DrJill:** Let's talk about those action words.
**DrJill:** You know?
**DrJill:** {Verbs|動詞|/vɜːrbz/|Verbs describe actions.}
**DrJill:** When we finish some {actions|行動/動作|/ˈækʃənz/|We take actions every day.}, we add an ED to the end.
**DrJill:** For example, I am {dancing|踊っている|/ˈdænsɪŋ/|I am dancing at the party.}
**DrJill:** I am done.
**DrJill:** I {danced|踊った|/dæn(t)st/|I danced all night.}
**DrJill:** Next, Anna {plays|遊ぶ/プレイする|/pleɪz/|Kids play in the park.} a game at a {playground|遊び場|/ˈpleɪɡraʊnd/|Children love the playground.}.
**DrJill:** She gets a list of actions and how to do them.
**DrJill:** To win, she needs to do the actions in {order|順序|/ˈɔːrdər/|Put things in order.}
**DrJill:** Now let's join Anna.
**DrJill:** Listen for words like {around|周りに/まわりに|/əˈraʊnd/|Walk around the park.}, under, up
**DrJill:** Through and between.
**DrJill:** Also listen for words ending in E B.
**Anna:** Max, Daisy, we are at the playground.
**Anna:** We are ready to play.
**Anna:** Max and Daisy are watching from home.
**Anna:** Felix, you read the actions.
**Students:** First, walk {around|周りに/まわりに|/əˈraʊnd/|Walk around the park.} the dragonfly.
**Students:** Second, {crawl|這う/匍匐する|/krɔːl/|Babies crawl before walking.} under the slide.
**Students:** Third, {climb|登る|/klaɪm/|Climbers climb mountains.} up the tree.
**Students:** Fourth, {crawl|這う/匍匐する|/krɔːl/|Babies crawl before walking.} through
**Students:** a log and fifth {cross|横切る/渡る|/krɔːs/|Cross the street carefully.} the finish line between the trees.
**Students:** On your mark get set, go
**Max:** Anna is walking around the dragonfly.
**Max:** She is {rolling|転がる|/ˈroʊlɪŋ/|The ball is rolling.} under the slide.
**Max:** Now she is climbing up the tree.
**Max:** She is {crawling|這っている|/ˈkrɔːlɪŋ/|Babies are crawling.} through a log.
**Anna:** Um Felix, I think I'm {stuck|立ち往生した/詰まった|/stʌk/|The car is stuck in mud.}
**Anna:** I did it.
**Students:** Good job, Mom, but you missed one
**DrJill:** What?
**DrJill:** No!
**DrJill:** Anna {missed|逃した/見落とした|/mɪst/|I missed the bus this morning.} an action!
**DrJill:** Which one?
**DrJill:** Can you {remember|思い出す/覚えている|/rɪˈmɛmbər/|Do you remember my birthday?}?
**DrJill:** Let's find out.
**Anna:** I {walked|歩いた|/wɔːkt/|I walked to the park.}, {climbed|登った|/klaɪmd/|He climbed the mountain.}, rolled, crawled.
**Anna:** Oh wait, that is only four.
**Anna:** Oh, I {forgot|忘れた|/fərˈɡɑːt/|I forgot my keys at home.} to cross the {finish line|ゴールライン|/ˈfɪnɪʃ laɪn/|He crossed the finish line first.} between those two trees.
**Max:** He wants to the finish line.
**DrJill:** Try this {game|ゲーム/遊び|/ɡeɪm/|Let's play a fun game.} with your friends.
**DrJill:** Make a list of {actions|行動/動作|/ˈækʃənz/|Show me your actions.} in English.
**DrJill:** Remember them and then do them.
**DrJill:** And come see us again on Let's Learn English with Anna.
